posts.content:
We're opening 3 summer internship spots for backend engineers. If you've shipped something real and enjoy working close to the metal — apply. We move fast and give interns real ownership.

👇 Link in the Jobs tab.

comments.content: Just applied! Love what you all are building with the CI pipeline tooling.

posts.content: Shipped my final project for Advanced Systems this semester — a distributed key-value store written in Go with Raft consensus. 4 weeks, a lot of late nights, and exactly zero memory leaks (eventually).

comments.content: This is impressive work — have you considered Go for backend roles? We use it heavily.

posts.content: We just published our 2026 Climate Tech Talent Report. One finding that stood out: 68% of climate startups say hiring is their #1 bottleneck. Platforms like this one are part of the solution.

linkPreview.title: 2026 Climate Tech Talent Report — GreenPulse
linkPreview.description: Key hiring trends, salary benchmarks, and the skills gap facing climate-focused companies.

posts.content:
Hot take: most onboarding flows treat first-time users like they already know what the product does. The best ones I've seen start with a single question — 'what are you trying to accomplish?' — and build from there.

What's the most thoughtful onboarding you've encountered?

comments.content: Notion's early onboarding was the gold standard for us — they got out of the way immediately.

posts.content:
Built a dashboard in a weekend that tracks Austin's real-time air quality + overlays it against traffic and weather data. Surprised by how strongly particulate matter correlates with Tuesday morning rush hour specifically.

Open-sourcing it next week.

We crossed 4,000 teams on Stackform this month. When we started, our bet was that internal tooling was broken in a way nobody wanted to talk about. Turns out, a lot of people wanted to talk about it.

Huge thanks to the engineering team and to every team that gave us candid feedback early.

comments.content: Big congrats! Stackform's component library was genuinely the best DX I've used for internal tools.

communities.name: Power Electronics Society
communities.description: The hub for students and professionals working on power conversion, motor drives, renewable energy integration, and high-voltage systems. Weekly paper reviews and lab share sessions.

communities.name: PCB Design Guild
communities.description: Share your PCB layouts, get design-rule feedback, and discuss advanced topics like signal integrity, impedance control, and HDI stackups. Monthly gerber review nights.

communities.name: Embedded & IoT Builders
communities.description: From bare-metal RTOS to Linux on a Pi — this is the space for hands-on embedded developers. Share projects, debug sessions, and firmware tips.

communities.name: RF & Wireless Engineers
communities.description: Antenna design, RF front-ends, spectrum analysis, Bluetooth/WiFi/5G. A technically focused group with a low tolerance for vague questions — come prepared.

communities.name: Würth Prototyping Circle
communities.description: Exclusive to students using Würth Elektronik components through the academic access programme. Share designs, ask component questions, and get feedback from Würth engineers.

communities.name: Women in EE & Tech
communities.description: A community for women and non-binary engineers in electrical engineering and related fields. Peer support, career advice, and celebrating wins — large and small.

communities.name: EV & E-Mobility Hackers
communities.description: Battery management systems, motor controllers, charging infrastructure, and vehicle-to-grid. If it moves electrons to move vehicles, it belongs here.

communities.name: AI Hardware Accelerators
communities.description: FPGA, custom ASICs, GPU microarchitecture, and the hardware powering modern AI workloads. Deep technical discussions, paper reviews, and silicon bringup stories.

events.title: GaN vs SiC Selection Workshop
events.description: Interactive workshop comparing GaN and SiC devices for 48V-bus applications. Includes live simulation and a Q&A with Prof. Hartmann.

events.title: FreeRTOS Task Scheduling Deep Dive
events.description: A technical walkthrough of FreeRTOS task priorities, pre-emption, and common pitfalls. Live debugging session with a STM32 target board.

events.title: EV Battery Pack Design Hackathon
events.description: 48-hour virtual hackathon: design a 72V 20Ah pack for an electric cargo bike. Teams of 2–4. Judged on energy density, safety design, and cost estimate.

events.title: PCB Layout Review Night #12
events.description: Submit your PCB layout before the event; community members give structured, constructive feedback. All skill levels welcome.

events.title: FPGA Acceleration for Neural Networks — Industry Talk
events.description: Engineer from Xilinx/AMD presents real-world FPGA deployment patterns for ML inference. Followed by open Q&A.

events.title: Würth Components Office Hours
events.description: Monthly drop-in with Würth Elektronik application engineers. Bring your design questions, datasheet confusions, or component selection dilemmas.

news.title: TSMC Announces 1.4nm "A14" Process Node, Targeting Mass Production in 2028
news.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company has unveiled its next-generation 1.4nm process node, dubbed A14, featuring gate-all-around (GAA) transistor architecture. The company claims a 15% performance improvement and 30% power reduction over N2, with apple silicon and HPC clients expected to be first adopters.

news.title: Infineon Launches CoolGaN 650V Devices Targeting Datacentre PSUs
news.summary: Infineon Technologies has expanded its CoolGaN portfolio with new 650V GaN transistors optimised for AC-DC power conversion in server power supply units. The devices achieve 99% efficiency at 12V bus voltage and are available in compact SMD packages suitable for high-density power stages.

news.title: Nordic Semi Unveils nRF54L Series: Ultra-Low-Power BLE 6.0 + Matter SoCs
news.summary: Nordic Semiconductor has announced the nRF54L series, the industry's first SoCs with Bluetooth LE 6.0 and native Matter support in a sub-mW active current envelope. The devices introduce a heterogeneous dual-core architecture pairing a 128MHz Arm Cortex-M33 with a dedicated Matter accelerator core.

news.title: KiCad 9.0 Released: Differential Pair Router Overhaul and 3D STEP Export
news.summary: The KiCad EDA team has released version 9.0, featuring a completely rewritten differential pair router with length-matching tuning, push-and-shove improvements, and native STEP assembly export for full 3D manufacturing review. The update also brings a new constraint-driven DRC engine.

news.title: Qualcomm X85 Modem Achieves 10 Gbps Peak 5G Download in FR2 mmWave Tests
news.summary: Qualcomm's Snapdragon X85 baseband has set a new commercial modem record, reaching 10.4 Gbps peak downlink throughput in millimetre-wave (FR2) testing. The device integrates advanced carrier aggregation across 8 mmWave carriers and is expected to debut in flagship smartphones Q3 2026.

news.title: Bosch and Continental Partner on 800V Silicon Carbide Traction Inverter Platform
news.summary: Bosch Mobility and Continental AG have announced a joint development agreement for a shared 800V SiC traction inverter platform targeting mid-range EVs. The platform will use dual-sided liquid cooling and targets 98.5% peak efficiency, with SOP planned for 2028 across multiple OEM programmes.

news.title: Google TPU v6 "Trillium" Benchmarks Published: 5x Compute vs v4 per Chip
news.summary: Google has published detailed performance benchmarks for its sixth-generation Tensor Processing Unit, codenamed Trillium. The chips deliver 4.7x peak compute improvement and 2x memory bandwidth versus TPU v4, using a new systolic array architecture with in-package HBM3e and custom optical interconnects.

news.title: EU Chips Act: €43B Fund Unlocks First Semiconductor Manufacturing Grants
news.summary: The European Commission has announced the first disbursement of grants under the EU Chips Act, totalling €8.2 billion directed at Intel's Magdeburg fab, TSMC's Dresden joint venture, and STMicroelectronics' Crolles expansion. The grants aim to double Europe's semiconductor market share to 20% by 2030.

news.title: TI Releases High-Density LLC Resonant Controller with 99.2% Efficiency at 3.3 kW
news.summary: Texas Instruments has introduced the UCC256xxA series LLC resonant controller IC, capable of driving 3.3 kW LLC converters with 99.2% peak efficiency. The device integrates burst-mode control for light-load efficiency, adaptive dead-time optimisation, and protection features compliant with IEC 62368-1.

news.title: Raspberry Pi Compute Module 5 Released with PCIe 3.0 and LPDDR5
news.summary: Raspberry Pi Ltd has launched the Compute Module 5 featuring the BCM2712 quad-core Cortex-A76 SoC, up to 8GB LPDDR5, 64GB eMMC, and a native PCIe 3.0 x1 lane. The CM5 targets industrial and embedded compute applications where the PCIe lane enables NVMe storage and custom peripheral attachment.

news.title: Wolfspeed Opens World's Largest SiC Wafer Fab in Siler City, NC
news.summary: Wolfspeed has inaugurated its "The JP" mega-factory in Siler City, North Carolina — the world's largest dedicated SiC wafer manufacturing facility. The 445,000 sq ft facility targets 200mm wafer production at scale, supporting the explosive demand from EV traction, industrial motor drives, and grid infrastructure.

news.title: New IEC 61000-4-39 Standard Defines Conducted RF Immunity Test for EV Chargers
news.summary: The International Electrotechnical Commission has finalised IEC 61000-4-39, establishing standardised conducted RF immunity test procedures specifically for electric vehicle charging equipment. The standard covers conducted disturbances in the 150 kHz–30 MHz range and is mandatory for CE marking in the EU from 2027.

threads.question: Why does my I²C sensor stop responding after running for ~30 minutes? Using STM32 + BMP280.
threads.nexusResponse:
Hey @TechLena — great question, and you're definitely not alone on this one! Based on what our community solved last month, this is a classic symptom of one of three things. Let's figure this out together:

**🔍 Most Likely Causes:**

**1. Clock Stretching Timeout** — The STM32 HAL I²C driver has a default timeout. If the BMP280 stretches the clock longer than expected during a conversion, the peripheral can lock up silently. Try increasing your HAL_I2C_Master_Transmit timeout parameter.

**2. Missing or Weak Pull-up Resistors** — I²C lines need external pull-ups (typically 4.7kΩ for 3.3V systems). Signal integrity degrades under thermal load — which explains the consistent 30-minute mark.

**3. Bus Lock-up After NACK** — If a transmission is interrupted, the bus can lock. Add a software I²C bus recovery routine — toggle SCL 9 times to flush any stuck slave.

**🛠 Quick Fix to try first:**
HAL_I2C_DeInit(&hi2c1); → HAL_I2C_Init(&hi2c1);

This forces a peripheral reset without a full system reboot. Many community members resolved the 30-minute lockup with exactly this.

Has anyone else tackled I²C lockup on STM32 recently? Drop your fix below! 👇
threads.ctaForAuthor: Hey @TechLena! You've asked your first great question 🌱 Answer one peer question this week to unlock your 🧠 Curious Mind badge (+50 rep). Want me to find a thread you can help with?

threads.question: Best approach for EMI shielding on a 4-layer PCB with a switching regulator at 400kHz?
threads.nexusResponse:
Hey @CircuitSam — solid design question, and the 400kHz range is exactly where things get interesting. Based on what our community and Würth Elektronik's application notes have covered, here's the structured approach:

**🛡 Recommended Stackup:**
Layer 1 — Signal + Switching components (tight loops!)
Layer 2 — Ground plane (unbroken, directly beneath L1) ← Your primary EMI weapon
Layer 3 — Power plane
Layer 4 — Signal (low-speed, away from switching nodes)

**⚡ Key Layout Rules:**
• Keep the hot loop (switch node → inductor → output cap → GND) as small as possible
• Never route sensitive signals beneath the switching node
• Use a pour guard / keepout around the SW node on all layers
• Stitch vias around the board perimeter every λ/20

**🔩 Component Pick:**
Würth Elektronik's WE-MAPI and WE-SFIA series inductors are shielded and specifically characterized for this frequency range — a community favourite for this exact use case.

Any power layout veterans want to add their preferred stackup? The more real-world examples, the better! 👇

threads.question: What's the difference between a working student position and an internship at a tech company?
threads.nexusResponse:
Welcome to the community, @NewbieNick — and don't let anyone tell you this is a "basic" question. It trips a lot of people up outside the European tech ecosystem. Let's break it down clearly:

**🎓 Internship (Praktikum)**
• Fixed term, typically 3–6 months
• Full-time commitment (40hrs/week)
• Often mandatory as part of a degree programme
• Focused on a single project or rotation

**💼 Working Student (Werkstudent)**
• Ongoing, part-time role (15–20hrs/week)
• Must be actively enrolled as a student
• Can last 1–2+ years alongside your studies
• More responsibility, deeper team integration
• Often converts to a full-time offer upon graduation

**Which is better?** Depends on your goal. If you want depth and a long-term foot in the door — working student. If you want breadth and a structured sprint — internship.

Our Jobs board has both types listed from Würth Elektronik right now if you want to compare real postings side by side! 🔍

Has anyone here done both? Would love your comparison in the replies! 👇

weFeed.title: WE-FNCS — Nanocrystalline EMI Shielding Sheets
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik introduces nanocrystalline EMI shielding sheets. Quick and easy surface mounting with superior suppression performance at high frequencies — ideal for PCB-level EMI management.

weFeed.title: WPME-CDI2C — Digital Isolators for I²C Interfaces
weFeed.summary: New digital isolators from Würth Elektronik provide galvanic isolation of digital signals on I²C buses. Supports fast mode (400 kHz) and fast mode plus (1 MHz), with 5 kVrms isolation voltage. Suitable for industrial, medical, and automotive applications.

weFeed.title: WE-SFIA — SMT Flat-Wire Inductor for Automotive Electronics
weFeed.summary: The WE-SFIA SMT flat-wire power inductor is engineered for automotive DC-DC converters. Its flat-wire winding technology achieves ultra-low DCR and excellent thermal performance in an AEC-Q200-qualified package, meeting the rigorous demands of ADAS and powertrain applications.

weFeed.title: SMDC Horticulture LEDs — Expanded Portfolio for Plant Growth
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik expands its Horticulture LED portfolio with new SMDC emitters. More light output, greater efficiency, and better plant growth — the new wavelength options target red (660 nm) and far-red (730 nm) spectra for vertical farming and greenhouse applications.

weFeed.title: IC LEDs — Integrated Control Circuit LEDs Set New Standards
weFeed.summary: ICLEDs from Würth Elektronik integrate an intelligent control circuit directly into the LED package. Where traditional LED solutions reach their limits, ICLED's built-in dimming, colour mixing, and fault detection begin to shine. Targeted at signage, automotive interior, and industrial HMI.

weFeed.title: RoHS Lead-Free Transition — New Varistors & SMT Spacers
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik is implementing lead-free alternatives across its passive component portfolio in compliance with RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU. New lead-free varistors and SMT spacers are now available. Learn how to transition your BOM without compromising reliability.

weFeed.title: Intelligent Control Systems (ICS) — Vehicles & Industrial
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik ICS provides complete system solutions for construction, agricultural, and commercial vehicles. From power distribution and signal transmission to control & display — covering simple components through to full system integration.

weFeed.title: ICS Engineering Services — From Idea to Series Product
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik ICS offers unique engineering services from initial concept through prototype to series production. Experience their system design, testing, and qualification capabilities first-hand.

weFeed.title: WEsystems — Wire Bonding, PCBs & Custom System Processing
weFeed.summary: WEsystems combines wire bonding services, printed circuit board manufacturing, and customer-specific system processing under one roof. Würth Elektronik acts as customer advocate and solution provider throughout the full product lifecycle.

weFeed.title: Technical Support — Personal, Professional & On-Site
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik offers best-in-class technical support including FAE visits, application engineering, design-in assistance, REDEXPERT parametric selection tool, and online calculators for magnetics, power, and RF design.

weFeed.title: EcoVadis Platinum — Leading the Way in Sustainable PCB Manufacturing
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik Circuit Board Technology has achieved EcoVadis Platinum status — placing it in the top 1% of companies assessed globally for sustainability performance. The rating covers environment, labour & human rights, ethics, and sustainable procurement.

weFeed.title: How to Find an Innovative Employer? Würth Elektronik's Answer
weFeed.summary: What makes a company truly innovative — and how do you find one? Würth Elektronik shares its approach: R&D investment, know-how transfer through university partnerships, flat hierarchies, and an engineering-first culture. Insight for students evaluating employers.

weFeed.title: EN 45545-2 Railway Certification — Ready for Rail
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik PCBs are now certified to EN 45545-2 — the European fire protection standard for railway vehicles. Covers hazard levels HL1 through HL3 for materials used in rolling stock and infrastructure equipment.

weFeed.title: Small Component, Big Responsibility — The Truth About PCB Quality
weFeed.summary: PCBs are rarely the component engineers think hardest about — but they're often the root cause of field failures. Würth Elektronik's webinar with Dr. Andreas Schilpp explains IPC class, material selection, copper roughness, and why blind/buried via quality matters.

weFeed.title: Open Source Smartwatch Powered by ADVANCED.hdi PCB Technology
weFeed.summary: OV Tech's open-source smartwatch project uses Würth Elektronik's ADVANCED.hdi high-density interconnect PCBs. The design packs BLE, IMU, display driver, and 72-hour battery life into a 38×38mm board — a showcase of what HDI enables for wearable electronics.

weFeed.title: Asia Production by Würth Elektronik — More Than Just an Alternative
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik's Asia production network offers customers quality manufacturing with European engineering oversight and real logistics relief. Covers PCBs, assemblies, and complete system builds — reducing lead times while maintaining Würth quality standards.

weFeed.title: 10 Years of EMC Research — University of Valencia Partnership
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik and the University of Valencia celebrate a decade of collaboration in Electromagnetic Compatibility research. The Cátedra Würth has produced 40+ peer-reviewed publications, 12 PhD theses, and numerous EMC measurement tools freely available to the industry.

weFeed.title: WE United Leadership Summit — Reflections on Leading in Complex Tech Environments
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik hosted the WE United Leadership Summit in Munich, bringing together senior leaders across divisions to discuss navigating technological complexity, talent development, and the company's innovation roadmap for 2026–2030.

weFeed.title: Free Seminars — Electronics, EMC, PCB Design & More
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik runs free, expert-led seminars worldwide covering power electronics, EMC compliance, PCB design rules, RF fundamentals, and magnetics design. Available in-person and online. Register at no cost — suitable for students and working engineers.

weFeed.title: Würth Elektronik at PCIM Europe 2026 — Modern Power Electronics Solutions
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik will exhibit at PCIM Europe 2026 in Nuremberg (27–29 May). Visit Hall 9, Stand 540 to see live demos of new SiC gate drivers, resonant magnetics, GaN-compatible inductors, and the REDEXPERT simulation platform.

weFeed.title: Würth Elektronik ICS at iVT Expo 2026 — Intelligent Power Distribution
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik ICS exhibits at iVT International Expo 2026, showcasing intelligent power distribution solutions for off-highway vehicles. Live demos include solid-state relay modules, CANopen-controlled distribution boxes, and the new ICS SmartBox system.

weFeed.title: IKOM — TU Munich Career Forum (4 Annual Job Fairs)
weFeed.summary: IKOM organises four career job fairs per year at the Technical University of Munich. Connect directly with top-tier engineering companies including Würth Elektronik, Siemens, BMW, and MAN. Open to all students — free registration.

weFeed.title: About Würth Elektronik — Manufacturer of Electronic & Electromechanical Components
weFeed.summary: Würth Elektronik is a leading manufacturer of electrical and electromechanical components, printed circuit boards, and intelligent systems. Family-owned, headquartered in Waldenburg, Germany, with 7,800+ employees globally and free samples available for engineers.
